designAudit.notes: matched design tokens; fixed 2 spacing regressions during the audit

_field_notes.phase: explore | test | gate | evidence | done | blocked  (resume reads this)
_field_notes.kind: ui | automation | mixed  (drives testing strategy and walkthrough flow selection)
_field_notes.gates.<objective>.exit: process exit code; 0 == pass. The /goal evaluator can confirm gates from these without re-running them.
_field_notes.gates.codexReview.verdict: approve | approve-with-override | needs-attention. The Codex adversarial-review loop result. 'approve' == both models agree. 'approve-with-override' == Codex still flagged but Claude rebutted each finding as a false positive (logged in overrides[] + docs/decisions.md); recorded honestly, keeps the global gate out of clean 'passed'. Run Codex calls SERIALLY — concurrent codex exec revokes the shared OAuth token.
_field_notes.gates.codex*.by / .effort: model + reasoning effort actually used, for cost auditing. Defaults: review codex/gpt-5.4 effort=low, pwtest codex/gpt-5.4 effort=medium. Escalation (per references/codex-verification.md): review -> effort=medium on a material finding / high-risk slice / low-confidence first pass; pwtest -> gpt-5.5 effort=xhigh only on repeated unclear fails or a high-risk slice.
_field_notes.gates.codexPwTest.result: pass | fail  (Codex independently re-drove the running app through the acceptance with its own playwright skill — a second test pass that never saw Claude's committed test).
_field_notes.gates.designAudit.verdict: clean | issues  (subjective gate reduced to a written verdict + who produced it)
_field_notes.gates.video.status: verified | failed-but-unblocking | skipped. 'failed-but-unblocking' = walkthrough hit its retry ceiling (STUCK); log a blocker and CONTINUE, never wait for input.
_field_notes.gates.video.flagged: true if the run is an honest failure (the video shows it not working). An honestly-flagged run still records evidence; it does not fake success.
